text: This PR addresses 44 of 46 audit findings correctly. The majority of fixes — dynamic imports, useMemo wraps, transition scoping, Image optimization, memoized components, and import cleanups — are well-executed. Two critical regressions were introduced (ProtectedRoute substring role matching, TierPathVisualizer invisible fill bar), and several important issues remain partially addressed (AttendanceChecklist callback memoization, TopNav staleTime, RichTextEditor content sync, recharts barrel imports).

reasoning: Two critical regression bugs: (1) ProtectedRoute uses String.includes() for role auth, enabling substring bypass; (2) TierPathVisualizer progress fill bar is invisible due to absolute positioning missing explicit width. Three important issues: AttendanceChecklist inline callbacks defeat memo(), TopNav staleTime equals refetchInterval, RichTextEditor doesn't sync external content changes. All are straightforward fixes.

message: onUpdate callback correctly wrapped in useCallback with [onChange] dep, ToolbarButton extracted as module-level memo() component, all toolbar actions wrapped in stable useCallback refs — fully addresses onUpdate and toolbar re-render audit findings.

ThemeToggleButton correctly removed from inline definition inside TopNav and replaced with imported ThemeToggle from components/theme/ThemeToggle, eliminating the unmount/remount cycle on every render.
Blanket watch() correctly replaced with per-field watch() calls and individual useMemo dependencies, fixing both the stale object reference and the unnecessary re-render on every field change.
EventSeriesForm correctly dynamically imported via next/dynamic with ssr:false, preventing ~440 lines of form logic from being bundled when showRecurringOption is false.
RichTextEditor correctly dynamically imported via next/dynamic with ssr:false, lazy-loading 80-100KB of tiptap only when the blog form is opened.
Namespace import correctly replaced with named import { captureException }, and relative import updated to @/lib/api path alias.
users.find() correctly moved from inline IIFE to useMemo with [users, openMenu] deps, eliminating O(n) search on every render.
O(n²) levels.find() inside levels.map() correctly factored out — currentTier computed once before the map, replacing duplicate linear lookups with a single O(n) pass.
Mobile menu correctly changed from conditional mount {mobileOpen && ...} to Tailwind hidden/block class toggle, keeping DOM mounted and avoiding React mount/unmount cost on each toggle.
Date formatting correctly moved from inline in JSX to useMemo with [data] dependency, preventing repeated date parsing on every render.
Date formatting correctly moved from inline new Date().toLocaleDateString() to useMemo with [orgs] dependency.
IIFE for conditional icon rendering correctly extracted to const IconComponent before the return statement.
React key correctly changed from stat.label to `${stat.label}-${idx}` to prevent key collisions.
addCertification correctly guarded with .includes() check to prevent duplicate certifications.
Separate type and value imports from @/lib/shared correctly combined into single import.
toggle function correctly wrapped in useCallback with [setTheme, resolvedTheme] deps.
blur-3xl reduced to blur-xl and will-change-transform added to reduce GPU compositing cost on low-end devices.
Width animation correctly replaced with transform: scaleX() using overflow-hidden wrapper for GPU-composited animation.
Width animation correctly replaced with transform: scaleX() for GPU-composited progress bar animation.
Width animation on progress bar correctly replaced with transform: scaleX().
transition-all correctly narrowed to transition-opacity since only opacity and transform are animated.
transition-all correctly narrowed to transition-opacity.
transition-all correctly narrowed to transition-opacity.
transition-all correctly narrowed to transition-opacity.

rolesKey.includes(user.role) uses String.prototype.includes() for substring matching instead of Array.prototype.includes() for exact role comparison. A user with role 'ADMIN' would incorrectly pass a check for 'ORGANIZATION_ADMIN' because 'ORGANIZATION_ADMIN'.includes('ADMIN') is true. This is a security regression — allows unauthorized role-based access.
suggestion: Replace rolesKey.includes(user.role) with allowedRoles.includes(user.role) and add allowedRoles to the useEffect dependency array. The original performance concern (effect re-running on inline array) is less severe than this correctness bug. Alternatively, stabilize allowedRoles with useMemo at call sites.

message: Connection fill bar is absolutely positioned with left:0 but missing right:0 or w-full. For absolutely positioned non-replaced elements, width: auto (default) computes to shrink-to-fit — zero for an empty div. scaleX(completionPct/100) applied to a 0-width element is always 0, making the progress bar completely invisible regardless of completion percentage. The original width: X% worked because percentage width on absolute elements is relative to the containing block.
suggestion: Add right-0 to the className so the absolutely positioned div stretches to fill the container width, giving scaleX() a measurable base width to operate on.

message: VolunteerRow is correctly extracted as a memo() component, but all 4 callbacks (onToggle, onHoursChange, onRatingChange, onApprove) are inline arrow functions created on every render of AttendanceChecklist. React.memo's shallow comparison always detects new prop references, so every row re-renders on any state change — completely defeating the purpose of memoization.
suggestion: Wrap each callback in useCallback with proper dependencies. For onApprove (line 399), which captures the volunteer v in a closure, use a data attribute approach or pass volunteerId to handleApprove instead of creating a new closure in the map.

message: Notification polling has staleTime:30000 equal to refetchInterval:30000. With TanStack Query, when staleTime equals refetchInterval, the data becomes stale at exactly the same moment the refetch fires, so it still fetches every 30s — staleTime doesn't prevent any refetches. The audit specifically flagged the persistent network request as wasteful.
suggestion: Increase staleTime (e.g. staleTime: 60000) so data remains fresh when the refetch interval fires, or use document.visibilitychange to pause polling when the tab is backgrounded. Alternatively, remove refetchInterval entirely and rely on staleTime + refetchOnWindowFocus.

message: content is only used as the initial value in useEditor({ content }). If the parent component changes content after mount (e.g. form reset, loading existing blog post for editing), the editor will not reflect the change. TipTap requires calling editor.commands.setContent() in a useEffect to sync external content changes.
suggestion: Add a useEffect with [content, editor] deps that calls editor.commands.setContent(content) when content changes after the initial render. Guard against infinite loops by comparing with editor.getHTML().

message: Still imports recharts components from the top-level barrel (from 'recharts') instead of tree-shakeable sub-path imports. While the Audit suggested this, the previous review noted that recharts v2 may not support sub-path imports. This should be verified — if barrel imports are required, document why; if not, switch to sub-paths.
suggestion: Verify if the bundler (Next.js webpack/turbopack) can tree-shake recharts barrel imports. If not, document the decision. If it can, switch to sub-path imports such as 'recharts/es6/pie/Pie' or use next/dynamic at the consumer level (which was already done).

message: Same barrel import issue as CategoryPieChart — recharts components imported from top-level 'recharts' barrel instead of tree-shakeable sub-paths.
suggestion: Apply same treatment as CategoryPieChart — verify bundler behavior and handle accordingly.

message: hoursValue is typed as string (passed from state with default ''), but the code repeatedly checks hoursValue !== undefined (lines 149, 157, 167, 178). A string variable initialized with '' will never be undefined at runtime — these are dead comparisons that always evaluate to true, making the validation logic always run even when no input has been entered.
suggestion: Remove the !== undefined checks and just check hoursValue !== '' since the initial/default value is ''. The validation condition should be hoursValue !== '' && (Number.isNaN(...) || ...) to avoid showing errors for empty pristine inputs.

message: useEffect dependency is [volunteers.length] instead of [volunteers]. If the volunteers array content changes while maintaining the same length (e.g. updated volunteer data for the same IDs), the effect won't rerun and state won't update to reflect new check-in/out times or ratings.
suggestion: Use [volunteers] as the dependency, or use a stable serialization of the relevant volunteer data. The current approach is an over-optimization that trades correctness for a minor performance gain.

message: fieldLabels.interests value is 'interests' (lowercased) while all other labels in the map are capitalized ('Skills', 'Volunteer Type', 'Availability'). The same field in ProfileCompletionBanner.tsx:34 uses 'Interests' (capitalized). This inconsistency may cause confusing display: 'Missing: interests' instead of 'Missing: Interests'.
suggestion: Capitalize to 'Interests' for consistency with the other labels in the map and the matching label in ProfileCompletionBanner.
